memes.label: "Anastasia Joliffe"
memes.description: "Anastasia Joliffe, daughter of a lodging-house keeper, rises from social obscurity to become the betrothed of Lord Blandamer — her journey tracing the novel's central themes of class, legitimacy, and the transformative power of love."

memes.label: "Anastasia's Departure for London"
memes.description: "Anastasia Joliffe's departure for London following her engagement marks her transition from Cullerne obscurity to her future as Lady Blandamer."

memes.label: "Appendix: Tunes Played by the Chimes"
memes.description: "At 3 o’clock."

memes.label: "Bell Chimes"
memes.description: "The chimes of Saint Sepulchre's Church in Cullerne sound through The Nebuly Coat as a musical motif marking time, mortality, and the inexorable unfolding of fate."

memes.label: "Bellevue Lodge"
memes.description: "Bellevue Lodge, the Cullerne lodging house kept by Miss Euphemia Joliffe, is Westray's home during his time in Cullerne and the domestic setting where the novel's central mystery is most intimately guarded."

memes.label: "The Bermondsey Chime Tune"
memes.description: "The hymn tune 'Bermondsey' in D major, scored in SATB for chimes, is the most prominent of the melodies played by Saint Sepulchre's chimes — a recurring musical motif whose notation appears in the novel's appendix."

memes.label: "The Blandamer Family"
memes.description: "The Blandamer family, whose coat of arms gives the novel its title, represents the apex of Cullerne's social hierarchy — an aristocratic lineage whose attenuated legitimacy drives the novel's central mystery."

memes.label: "Canon Parkyn"
memes.description: "The Rector of Cullerne, Canon Parkyn is a clergyman whose enthusiasm for the restoration project is matched by his concern for social propriety and institutional authority."

memes.label: "The Clerk"
memes.description: "A legal clerk in Cullerne whose handling of documents related to the Blandamer inheritance connects the professional world of law to the personal drama of legitimacy and identity."

memes.label: "Cullerne Bay"
memes.description: "Cullerne Bay, where the sea meets the town and where Martin Joliffe's parents drowned, is the novel's site of maritime tragedy — a place where the indifferent forces of nature intersect with human fate."

memes.label: "Cullerne Grammar School"
memes.description: "Cullerne Grammar School, where Martin Joliffe was educated and earned his scholarship to Oxford, represents the pathway of merit that offered bright working-class boys a route out of Cullerne — a route that, for Martin, led ultimately to tragedy."

memes.label: "Cullerne Minster"
memes.description: "Cullerne Minster, the great medieval church whose restoration brings Westray to the town, is the novel's central location — a physical embodiment of centuries of history, faith, and architectural ambition, threatened by the structural weakness of its central tower."

memes.label: "Cullerne Parish"
memes.description: "The ecclesiastical parish of Cullerne, administered by Canon Parkyn, represents the institutional Church — its authority, its finances, and its complex relationship with the aristocratic patrons and working people who constitute its congregation."

memes.label: "Cullerne Road Station"
memes.description: "Cullerne Road, the wayside railway station that serves the town, is the threshold through which characters enter and leave Cullerne — a point of arrival and departure that marks the boundary between the town's enclosed world and the larger world beyond."

memes.label: "Cullerne"
memes.description: "Cullerne, the decaying seaport town where The Nebuly Coat is set, is a character in its own right — its empty houses, abandoned wharves, and faded prosperity creating the atmosphere of melancholy decline that pervades the novel's every scene."

memes.label: "Cullerne Wharf"
memes.description: "Cullerne Wharf, the abandoned port district where Westray first walks with Sharnall, embodies the town's commercial decay — its empty warehouses and rusting tramway lines testifying to a prosperity that has ebbed away."

memes.label: "Discovery of the Tower's Weakness"
memes.description: "Westray's discovery that the central tower of Cullerne Minster rests on inadequate foundations introduces the novel's central architectural crisis."

memes.label: "Duty and Obligation"
memes.description: "Duty in The Nebuly Coat operates as the moral framework within which characters navigate competing claims — Westray's professional duty to the church, Lord Blandamer's hereditary duty to his lineage, and the quieter duties of kinship and care that bind the novel's domestic world."

memes.label: "Edward Westray"
memes.description: "Edward Westray, a young architect sent to superintend the restoration of Cullerne Minster, serves as the novel's protagonist, moral centre, and the reader's proxy — an outsider whose professional duty gradually entangles him in the town's web of secrets, genealogical mysteries, and moral obligations he never sought."

memes.label: "Epilogue"
memes.description: "On the same evening Lieutenant Ennefer, R.N., sailed down Channel in the corvette *Solebay*, bound for the China Station."

memes.label: "Family and Kinship"
memes.description: "Family in The Nebuly Coat is the central axis around which identity, inheritance, and obligation revolve — from the aristocratic Blandamers to the struggling Joliffes, blood ties determine destiny."

memes.label: "Fate and Fortune"
memes.description: "Fate in The Nebuly Coat manifests as the inescapable convergence of past and present — the Blandamer inheritance, the structural weakness of the tower, and the inexorable unfolding of consequences set in motion long before the novel's present."

memes.label: "The Father Smith Organ"
memes.description: "The organ of Cullerne Minster, built by the celebrated organ-maker 'Father' Smith, is a fine instrument fallen into decay — its neglected state mirroring the condition of its player, Mr. Sharnall."

memes.label: "Fording"
memes.description: "Fording, the stately country house of the Blandamer family, represents aristocratic wealth and hereditary power — visible from Cullerne on a clear day, yet remote and inaccessible to the town's ordinary inhabitants."

memes.label: "Guilt and Conscience"
memes.description: "Guilt in The Nebuly Coat operates on multiple levels — the architectural guilt of neglecting the central tower, the moral guilt of concealed crimes, and the psychological guilt that haunts characters who carry hidden knowledge."

memes.label: "The Hand of God"
memes.description: "The Hand of God inn, Cullerne's principal tavern, is the site of working-class conviviality and the circulation of local knowledge — a space where the Blandamer legend is told and retold among the town's ordinary inhabitants."

memes.label: "Janaway"
memes.description: "Janaway is a Cullerne workman whose practical knowledge of the church building and its history makes him a valuable informant for Westray's investigation of the tower."

memes.label: "The Joliffe Family"
memes.description: "The Joliffe family — Martin the obsessed genealogist, Anastasia the future Lady Blandamer, and Miss Euphemia the guardian of family secrets — represents the novel's central domestic unit, bound by love, loyalty, and the spectre of the nebuly coat."

memes.label: "Knowledge and Discovery"
memes.description: "The acquisition of knowledge structures The Nebuly Coat — Westray's architectural investigation of the tower parallels his gradual discovery of Cullerne's human secrets, both forms of knowledge carrying the burden of responsibility."

memes.label: "Lord Blandamer"
memes.description: "Lord Blandamer, the enigmatic peer whose arrival in Cullerne sets the novel's central mystery in motion, embodies the ambiguous power of inherited wealth, the weight of family secrets, and the uneasy relationship between aristocracy and obligation."

memes.label: "Lord Blandamer's Arrival"
memes.description: "Lord Blandamer's dramatic arrival at Cullerne Minster — revealing his identity to a startled verger with the words 'I am Lord Blandamer' — is the novel's inciting event, transforming the social landscape of Cullerne and setting in motion every subsequent plot development."

memes.label: "Lord Blandamer's Arrival in Cullerne"
memes.description: "Lord Blandamer's dramatic appearance at Cullerne Minster — sitting in the Fording pews and revealing his identity only when challenged — marks the moment when the novel's central mystery acquires a living embodiment."

memes.label: "Lord Blandamer's Confession"
memes.description: "Lord Blandamer's private revelations to Westray at Fording transform the architect from observer to reluctant keeper of dangerous secrets."

memes.label: "Marriage"
memes.description: "Marriage in The Nebuly Coat functions as the primary mechanism of social mobility and the resolution of the inheritance plot, culminating in the engagement between Lord Blandamer and Anastasia Joliffe."

memes.label: "Martin Joliffe"
memes.description: "Martin Joliffe, the half-brother of Anastasia whose obsessive belief that he was the rightful Blandamer heir drove him to madness and death, is the novel's tragic figure — a man destroyed by the genealogical secret at the heart of the nebuly coat."

memes.label: "Martin Joliffe's Genealogy"
memes.description: "Martin Joliffe's handwritten genealogy of the Blandamer family — elaborated and corrected through years of obsessive research — is the documentary heart of the novel's mystery."

memes.label: "Martin Joliffe's Madness and Death"
memes.description: "Martin Joliffe's obsessive belief that he was the rightful Blandamer heir — and his subsequent madness and death — is the tragic backstory that drives the novel's inheritance mystery."

memes.label: "The Mason"
memes.description: "The mason working on the Cullerne Minster restoration represents the practical craft knowledge that complements Westray's professional architectural expertise."

memes.label: "Memory and the Past"
memes.description: "Memory in The Nebuly Coat is the mechanism by which the past exerts its claim upon the present — through the physical memory inscribed in the church's stones, the genealogical memory preserved in documents, and the personal memories that haunt the novel's characters."

memes.label: "Miss Euphemia Joliffe"
memes.description: "Miss Euphemia Joliffe, keeper of the lodging house at Bellevue Lodge and guardian of her brother Martin's papers, is the novel's quiet anchor — a woman whose fierce family loyalty and domestic integrity hold the key to the Blandamer mystery."

memes.label: "Money and Wealth"
memes.description: "Money in The Nebuly Coat is the hidden force behind every social relationship, from the restoration fund that brings Westray to Cullerne to the Blandamer fortune that drives the inheritance plot."

memes.label: "Mr. Sharnall"
memes.description: "Mr. Sharnall, the embittered organist of Cullerne Minster, serves as Westray's guide to the town's history and the novel's most articulate critic of wealth and class privilege."

memes.label: "The Nebuly Coat"
memes.description: "The heraldic coat of arms of the Blandamer family — barry nebuly of six, argent and vert — a central symbol of lineage, obsession, and fate in John Meade Falkner's novel The Nebuly Coat."

memes.label: "The Nebuly Coat Window"
memes.description: "The great stained-glass window in the south transept of Cullerne Minster, created by Van Linge to the order of the third Lord Blandamer, is the novel's central artwork — a visual repository of Blandamer heraldry and history."

memes.label: "Prologue"
memes.description: "Sir George Farquhar, Baronet, builder of railway-stations, and institutes, and churches, author, antiquarian, and senior partner of Farquhar and Farquhar, leant back in his office chair and turned it sideways to give more point to his remarks."

memes.label: "Restoration"
memes.description: "The architectural restoration of Cullerne Minster serves as the narrative framework of The Nebuly Coat, bringing the young architect Westray to the town and setting in motion the novel's central conflicts over heritage, authenticity, decay, and the tension between preserving the past and confronting its hidden truths."

memes.label: "The Restoration Commission"
memes.description: "The commission to restore Cullerne Minster brings architect Edward Westray to the town, setting the entire plot in motion."

memes.label: "Decay and Ruin"
memes.description: "Decay in The Nebuly Coat is both physical and social — the crumbling fabric of Cullerne Minster mirrors the decline of the town, the attenuation of the Blandamer line, and the broader Victorian anxiety about the erosion of traditional order."

memes.label: "Saint Sepulchre's Church"
memes.description: "Saint Sepulchre's, the abandoned church in old Cullerne whose chimes continue to play though its congregation has vanished, embodies the novel's preoccupation with decay, persistence, and the survival of beauty amid neglect."

memes.label: "Sea and the Maritime"
memes.description: "The sea pervades The Nebuly Coat as both physical setting and symbolic force — Cullerne's history as a seaport, the salt-laden winds that sweep the church, and the maritime imagery woven through the novel's language."

memes.label: "Secrecy and Concealment"
memes.description: "Secrecy drives the narrative engine of The Nebuly Coat — from the hidden Blandamer painting to the concealed identity of Lord Blandamer, the novel is structured around the gradual revelation of buried truths."

memes.label: "Sharnall's Diatribe Against Wealth"
memes.description: "Mr. Sharnall's furious outburst against the Blandamers and the worship of wealth is the novel's most explicit statement of social criticism."

memes.label: "Sir George Farquhar"
memes.description: "Sir George Farquhar serves as consulting architect for the Cullerne restoration, representing the established professional authority against which Westray's growing independence is measured."

memes.label: "Social Class"
memes.description: "The rigid class hierarchy of Victorian England shapes every relationship in The Nebuly Coat, from the deference shown to Lord Blandamer to the social transformation of Anastasia Joliffe upon her engagement."

memes.label: "Standard Ebooks"
memes.description: "Standard Ebooks, the publisher of the digital edition used as the wiki's source text, represents the contemporary infrastructure of textual preservation that makes the novel accessible to modern readers."

memes.label: "The Architectural Firm"
memes.description: "The London architectural firm that employs Westray and Sir George Farquhar represents the professional world from which Westray comes and to which he will return — the modern infrastructure of expertise that the restoration project requires."

memes.label: "The Avenue at Fording"
memes.description: "The long avenue leading to Fording House, with its great gates bearing the nebuly coat, is a passage through landscaped space that marks the transition from the ordinary world to the domain of aristocratic power."

memes.label: "The Thirty-Year Silence of the Bells"
memes.description: "The silencing of Cullerne Minster's great bells — un-rung for thirty years due to fears of tower collapse — is a decades-long event that measures the tower's slow deterioration."
aliases: The Thirty-Year Silence of the Bells

memes.label: "The Blandamer Family"
memes.description: "The Blandamer family, lords of Fording and patrons of Cullerne Minster, is the aristocratic dynasty at the centre of The Nebuly Coat's inheritance plot — a lineage whose heraldic emblem (the nebuly coat) is as clouded by genealogical ambiguity as it is resplendent in silver and green."

memes.label: "The Portrait of the Old Lord Blandamer"
memes.description: "The portrait of the previous Lord Blandamer at Fording, whose penetrating grey eyes seem to watch the novel's climactic scenes, represents the persistence of the past into the present."
aliases: The Portrait of the Old Lord Blandamer

memes.label: "The Blandamer Tenantry"
memes.description: "The tenant farmers and estate workers who depend on the Blandamer family for their livelihoods represent the economic relationships that underpin aristocratic power."

memes.label: "The Drowning at Cullerne Bay"
memes.description: "The drowning of Martin Joliffe's parents in Cullerne Bay is the foundational tragedy of the Joliffe family — an event whose consequences ripple through the novel."

memes.label: "The Engagement of Lord Blandamer and Anastasia Joliffe"
memes.description: "The engagement between Lord Blandamer and Anastasia Joliffe reconfigures the novel's social landscape, transforming a lodging-house keeper's daughter into a future peeress."
aliases: The Engagement of Lord Blandamer and Anastasia Joliffe

memes.label: "The Father Smith Organ"
memes.description: "The Father Smith organ of Cullerne Minster — a sweet-voiced instrument built by the legendary organ-builder Father Smith — is the novel's central musical artefact, embodying the themes of craftsmanship, decay, and the tension between preservation and replacement that pervade the restoration plot."

memes.label: "The Joliffe Family"
memes.description: "The Joliffe family of Cullerne — lodging-house keepers, churchwardens, and organists — represents the novel's portrait of provincial trade-class life, a family whose obscure genealogy proves to be fatally entangled with the aristocratic Blandamer line."

memes.label: "The Examination of Martin Joliffe's Papers"
memes.description: "Westray's study of Martin Joliffe's genealogical papers reveals the depth of the dead man's obsession and provides crucial evidence for the Blandamer mystery."
aliases: The Examination of Martin Joliffe's Papers

memes.label: "The Library"
memes.description: "The library at Cullerne, where Westray consults books on heraldry and local history, represents the documentary resources through which the Blandamer mystery can be investigated — a repository of official knowledge."

memes.label: "The Marketplace"
memes.description: "Cullerne's marketplace is the town's public arena, where the community gathers and where the novel's climactic events draw the collective gaze of the townspeople."

memes.label: "The Nebuly Coat"
memes.description: "The nebuly coat — the barry nebuly of six, argent and vert — is the heraldic emblem of the Blandamer family and the novel's central symbolic image, representing the entangled themes of inheritance, identity, and the weight of the past that pervade The Nebuly Coat."

memes.label: "The Nebuly Coat Window"
memes.description: "The Nebuly Coat Window — the great stained-glass window in the south transept of Cullerne Minster depicting the Blandamer arms in sea-green and silver — is the novel's central visual artefact, a work of medieval craftsmanship that embodies the heraldic identity at the heart of the inheritance plot."

memes.label: "The Organ Loft"
memes.description: "The organ loft of Cullerne Minster, Mr. Sharnall's domain, is a space of music, bitterness, and privileged observation — high above the congregation, looking out onto the nebuly coat in the transept window."

memes.label: "The Secret of the Painting"
memes.description: "Westray's recognition that the painting in Miss Joliffe's house conceals the nebuly coat — the visual proof of the Blandamer inheritance claim — is the novel's crucial moment of revelation."

memes.label: "The Sexton"
memes.description: "The sexton of Cullerne Minster is a repository of local knowledge about the church, its bells, and the Blandamer family — a minor figure whose long memory makes him a crucial witness to the tower's history."

memes.label: "The Tower Crisis"
memes.description: "The climactic crisis in which the central tower's structural weakness threatens catastrophic collapse, forcing the novel's conflicts to their resolution."

memes.label: "The Tower Crisis"
memes.description: "The Tower Crisis — the growing structural threat posed by the unstable central tower of Cullerne Minster — is the novel's central engineering drama, pitting professional integrity against institutional denial and serving as the symbolic analogue to the genealogical instability at the heart of the Blandamer inheritance."

memes.label: "The Church Vaults"
memes.description: "The vaults beneath Cullerne Minster, which the Rector insists on having cemented despite Sharnall's objections, represent the hidden spaces beneath the church — both physical and metaphorical — that the novel's plot uncovers."

memes.label: "Time and Temporality"
memes.description: "Time in The Nebuly Coat is measured by the chimes of Saint Sepulchre, inscribed in the ancient stones of the minster, and felt in the slow decay of Cullerne's buildings — a force that connects the medieval past to the Edwardian present."

memes.label: "Westray's Final Departure"
memes.description: "Westray's departure from Cullerne at the novel's end marks the completion of his professional commission and his personal entanglement with the town's secrets."

memes.label: "Westray's First Walk Through Cullerne"
memes.description: "Westray's nocturnal walk through old Cullerne with Sharnall establishes the novel's atmosphere of decay and introduces the town's landscape."
